1851-MN002A : special neonatal care package: babies that required admission to sncu or nicu: babies admitted for short term care for conditions like: â€¢ mild respiratory distress/tachypnea â€¢ mild encephalopathy â€¢ severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy â€¢ haemorrhagic disease of new-born â€¢ unwell baby requiring monitoring â€¢ some dehydration â€¢ hypoglycaemia mother's stay and food in the hospital for breastfeeding, family centred care and (kangaroo mother care) kmc is mandatory and included in the package rate |
neonates  between 1500-1799g  or neonates of any weight with mild illness like, but not limited to: â€¢ mild respiratory distress/tachypnea â€¢ mild encephalopathy â€¢ severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy â€¢ unwell baby requiring monitoring â€¢ some dehydration â€¢ hypoglycaemia the above list is illustrative but not limited to these conditions. the required procedures may include radiant warmer care, gavage feeding, oxygen therapy, iv fluid administration, blood transfusion. the package includes food and stay for the mother in the hospital for breastfeeding, kangaroo mother care (kmc) and family centered care.[regular procedure]

1851-MN003A : intensive neonatal care package babies with birthweight 1500-1799 g or babies of any birthweight and at least one of the following conditions: â€¢ need for mechanical ventilation for less than 24 hours or non-invasive respiratory support (cpap, hffnc) â€¢ sepsis / pneumonia without complications â€¢ hyperbilirubinemia requiring exchange transfusion â€¢ seizures â€¢ major congenital malformations (pre-surgical stabilization, not requiring ventilation) â€¢ cholestasis significant enough requiring work up and in-hospital management â€¢ congestive heart failure or shock mother's stay and food in the hospital for breastfeeding, family centred care and (kangaroo mother care) kmc is mandatory and included in the package rate |
neonates of  any birthweight  with moderate illness like â€¢ need for mechanical ventilation for less than 24 hours or non-invasive respiratory support (cpap, hffnc) â€¢ sepsis / pneumonia without complications â€¢ hyperbilirubinemia requiring exchange transfusion â€¢ seizures requring anti-convulsants â€¢ major congenital malformations (pre-surgical stabilization) â€¢ cholestasis  requiring investigations and in-hospital management â€¢ congestive heart failure or shock the above list is illustrative but not limited to these conditions. the package includes mandatory stay and food of the mother in the hospital for breastfeeding, kangaroo mother care (kmc) and family centered care |
6000 /day |
